steps.action: Kecak (also spelled kécak, ketjak and kechak; Ba with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Kecak (also spelled kécak, ketjak and kechak; Ba with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: Since its creation, it has been performed primar
steps.text: Since its creation, it has been performed primarily by men, with the first women's kecak group having started in 2006.

steps.action: The dance is based on the story of the Ramayana
steps.text: The dance is based on the story of the Ramayana and is traditionally performed in temples and villages across Bali, Indonesia.

steps.action: Also known as the Ramayana monkey chant, the dan
steps.text: Also known as the Ramayana monkey chant, the dance is performed by a circle of as many as 150 performers wearing checked cloths around their waists, percussively chanting "chak", and moving their hands and arms.

steps.action: Kecak has roots in sanghyang, a trance-inducing
steps.text: Kecak has roots in sanghyang, a trance-inducing exorcism dance.

steps.action: He adapted it as a drama based on the Hindu Rama
steps.text: He adapted it as a drama based on the Hindu Ramayana and including dance, intended for performance before Western tourist audiences.

steps.action: Walter Spies worked with Indonesian dancer Wayan
steps.text: Walter Spies worked with Indonesian dancer Wayan Limbak, who popularized the dance by arranging internationally touring performances by Balinese groups.

steps.action: They form concentric circles, in the middle of w
steps.text: They form concentric circles, in the middle of which is a traditional Balinese coconut oil lamp.

steps.action: First they move their bodies rhythmically to the
steps.text: First they move their bodies rhythmically to the left and to the right, chanting the words "chak ke-chak ke-chak ke-chak" continuously in coordinated harmony and beat, in slow rhythm.

steps.action: Gradually the rhythm speeds up and by turns they
steps.text: Gradually the rhythm speeds up and by turns they lift their hands, trembling, into the air.

steps.action: The dance is performed for dance-dramas and the
steps.text: The dance is performed for dance-dramas and the story presented is taken from the Ramayana Hindu epic.

steps.action: Kecak dance performances in Bali usually take pl
steps.text: Kecak dance performances in Bali usually take place daily in the evening (6 pm, Bali time) at Balinese Hindu temples such as Uluwatu Temple and Tanah Lot.

steps.action: There are also dance stages used exclusively for
steps.text: There are also dance stages used exclusively for kecak performances in Ubud, Garuda Wisnu Kencana, Batu Bulan, Pandawa beach and other places in Bali.

steps.action: Dancers usually come from local villagers of the
steps.text: Dancers usually come from local villagers of the surrounding area of the performance; they usually have a main job other than dancing which they finish before performing the kecak dance.

steps.action: The dancers' income from the dance usually comes
steps.text: The dancers' income from the dance usually comes from tickets sold to the spectators.

steps.action: The most popular destination for kechak dance pe
steps.text: The most popular destination for kechak dance performances is Uluwatu Temple.

steps.action: One individual is responsible for maintaining th
steps.text: One individual is responsible for maintaining the beat of the chant by chanting "po-po-po-po".

steps.action: There is also a man whose job is to sing during
steps.text: There is also a man whose job is to sing during the chanting; he sings in a melodious or rhythmic tone according to the situation of the dance with vocalizations such as "Shiiir-yang-ngger-yang-nggur-yang-nggeer".

steps.action: Another man, known as the dalang, narrates the s
steps.text: Another man, known as the dalang, narrates the story during the dance, usually in Balinese and Sanskrit.

steps.action: The men chosen for these tasks are usually the s
steps.text: The men chosen for these tasks are usually the senior male dancers.

steps.action: The dancers who represent the core Ramayana char
steps.text: The dancers who represent the core Ramayana characters are considered an essential part of the dance.

steps.action: Rama, Sita, Lakshmana, and the Golden Deer, whos
steps.text: Rama, Sita, Lakshmana, and the Golden Deer, whose movements are gentle and smooth, are sometimes played by female dancers who are trained in such styles of movement.

steps.action: Here, the dancer playing Hanuman is blessed by a
steps.text: Here, the dancer playing Hanuman is blessed by a priest and enters a trance state for the fire kicking dance which follows.

steps.action: The dancer does not feel any pain from the fire
steps.text: The dancer does not feel any pain from the fire because he is in a state of trance.
